commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Missing icon/emoji for the current weather condition.
issues.fix: Add an icon or emoji prop for the current weather state to display alongside the temperature and condition text.

issues.description: Forecast item props are marked as optional without fallback values in the render logic.
issues.fix: Provide default values or ensure data presence before rendering forecast items to prevent empty text nodes.

evaluations.critique: The component is visually polished with excellent use of whitespace and typography hierarchy. However, it fails to include an icon for the current weather as requested in the prompt, focusing only on icons for the forecast strip.

evaluation.critique: The component has good overall structure with clear sections and proper use of grid/flex, but has several design token violations: hardcoded fallback colors in var() expressions (e.g., '#f4f4f5', '#18181b'), hardcoded padding/gap values as raw pixels (24, 16, 8, 4), and custom CSS with hardcoded border-radius instead of using design tokens. The forecast strip lacks proper scrolling behavior for many items and could benefit from wind speed unit flexibility.
